title: peucedanum arenarium var. neumayeri
intro: Peucedanum arenarium var. neumayeri, also known as Alpine Hog's Fennel, is a perennial herb belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae) and native to the high-altitude regions of the European Alps. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Peucedanum arenarium var. neumayeri include its potential to alleviate respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to soothe digestive issues and reduce fever. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used in folk medicine to treat a range of ailments.
